title: The Lost Jewel.
content:
I held a jewel in my fingers
    And went to sleep.
    The day was warm, and winds were prosy;
    I said: "'T will keep."

    I woke and chid my honest fingers, --
    The gem was gone;
    And now an amethyst remembrance
    Is all I own.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Lost Jewel."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
